CREATE TABLE test_table (a INTEGER, b INTEGER);
SELECT a, (a << 1) AS left_shifted FROM test_table;
SELECT b, (b << 3) AS left_shifted FROM test_table WHERE a = 3;
SELECT (SELECT a << 2 FROM test_table WHERE b = 4) AS shifted FROM test_table;
WITH shifted_values AS (SELECT a, (a << 2) AS left_shifted FROM test_table) SELECT * FROM shifted_values;
SELECT t1.a, t2.b, (t1.a << t2.b) AS combined_shift FROM test_table t1 JOIN test_table t2 ON t1.a < t2.a;
SELECT (5 << (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM test_table)) AS result;
CREATE TABLE second_table (c INTEGER, d INTEGER);
SELECT tt.a, (tt.a << st.d) AS shifted_result FROM test_table tt JOIN second_table st ON tt.a = st.c;
CREATE TABLE complex_table (id INTEGER, value INTEGER);
SELECT ct.id, (SELECT ct.value << 2 FROM complex_table ct WHERE ct.id = complex_table.id) AS shifted_value FROM complex_table;
